lede: A look at December 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Emily Dickinson and Ada Lovelace, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan
events.desc: Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan establishes the Marriage Fund in the United Arab Emirates in response to rising wedding prices.

events.title: Zanzibar
events.desc: Zanzibar gains independence from the United Kingdom as a constitutional monarchy, under Sultan Jamshid bin Abdullah.

events.title: United Kingdom
events.desc: British Prime Minister Winston Churchill receives the Nobel Prize in Literature.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Royal Navy capital ships HMS Prince of Wales and HMS Repulse are sunk by Imperial Japanese Navy torpedo bombers near British Malaya.

events.title: Brown Dog affair
events.desc: The worst night of the Brown Dog riots in London, when 1,000 medical students, protesting against the existence of a memorial for animals that have been vivisected, clash with 400 police officers.

events.title: Nguyễn Trung Trực
events.desc: Forces led by Nguyễn Trung Trực, an anti-colonial guerrilla leader in southern Vietnam, sink the French lorcha L'Esperance.

events.title: Encyclopædia Britannica
events.desc: The first edition of the Encyclopædia Britannica is published.

events.title: Isaac Newton
events.desc: Isaac Newton's derivation of Kepler's laws from his theory of gravity, contained in the paper De motu corporum in gyrum, is read to the Royal Society by Edmond Halley.

births.name: Melvil Dewey
births.line: American librarian, created the Dewey Decimal System

births.name: Harold Alexander, 1st Earl Alexander of Tunis
births.line: English field marshal and politician, 17th Governor General of Canada

births.name: Kenneth Branagh
births.line: British actor director, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Nelly Sachs
births.line: German-Swedish poet and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Jeff Garcia (comedian), American stand-up comedian and voice actor
Augusto Pinochet, Chilean general and dictator, 30th President of Chile
Luigi Pirandello, Italian dramatist, novelist, and poet Nobel Prize laureate
Alfred Nobel, Swedish chemist and engineer, invented Dynamite and founded the Nobel Prize
Leopold I of Belgium, King of the Belgians from 1831 to 1865

observances.text: Christian feast day: Behnam, Sarah, and the Forty Martyrs (Syriac Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Translation of the Holy House of Loreto
